description_long: This guard stone is a sculpted block of stone featuring a prominent relief of a bearded male head wearing a helmet, reminiscent of ancient Greek or Roman statuary. The head emerges from one end of a rectangular, block-like base, which is inscribed with text on its upper surface. The stone has a weathered, beige coloration, with subtle surface irregularities and signs of age. There is a small rectangular cutout or aperture on one side of the block, possibly for mounting or drainage. The overall form is solid and heavy, designed to serve as a protective barrier at the edge of a pathway or structure. The craftsmanship and inscription suggest historical or commemorative significance.
description: A weathered stone guard stone with a sculpted helmeted head and inscribed rectangular base, showing signs of age and historical detail.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the helmeted head and the rectangular base, with the inscription partially visible and a small cutout on the lower side.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the face of the helmeted head and the inscription on the top surface of the block.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the head in profile and the rectangular base, with the small cutout visible on this side as well.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the helmet and the unadorned end of the rectangular base, with the inscription less visible.
attribute_location: Typically found at the entrance to historical sites, along urban street corners, or adjacent to building facades in city centers, often marking boundaries or protecting structures from vehicle impact.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be installed fully above ground with the sculpted head facing outward, base flush with the ground surface.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible surface smoothing and minor chipping around the edges, especially on the helmet and corners of the base, likely caused by weathering, occasional vehicle contact, and foot traffic. Discoloration and patina are present due to prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_historical_markings: Inscription on the upper surface of the rectangular base, likely in a classical script, possibly commemorative or identifying in nature.
